title: dianthus seidlitzii
intro: Dianthus seidlitzii, also known as Balkan pink or Gypsy pink, is a perennial herb belonging to the carnation family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a digestive aid and to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, providing various health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Dianthus seidlitzii is often infused as a tea or used in tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
